mission.title: cert-manager: Add support for JKS, PKCS12 and PEM files
mission.description: Add support for JKS, PKCS12 and PEM files. Requested by 31+ users.

steps.title: Check current cert-manager deployment
steps.description:
Verify your cert-manager version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cert-manager
helm list -n cert-manager 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working cert-manager installation.

steps.title: Review cert-manager configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant cert-manager configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n cert-manager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cert-manager
kubectl get configmap -n cert-manager -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=cert-manager
```
It would be great if cert-manager supported different packaging formats, such as Java Keystore (https://www.digitalocean.com/community/tutorials/java-keytool-essentials-working-with-java-keystores),

steps.title: Apply the fix for Add support for JKS, PKCS12 and PEM files
steps.description:
This PR adds a new experimental flag to enable generating PKCS12 bundles in 'target secrets'.

This flag will be replaced with native support for PKCS12 bundles in future, once #2402 has been implemented.

/

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cert-manager
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Add support for JKS, PKCS12 and PEM files" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
This PR adds a new experimental flag to enable generating PKCS12 bundles in 'target secrets'.

This flag will be replaced with native support for PKCS12 bundles in future, once #2402 has been implemented.

/

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with cert-manager installed or the issue environment reproducible.
